strengths: 13+ years of full-stack engineering experience, well above the 6+ year requirement
Direct TypeScript/Node.js/React/MongoDB experience from Optilyz and current re:cinq role
Some exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at current employer
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere in the resume despite it being a primary stack requirement
No Kotlin experience and only listed Java exposure is from robotic-arm work nearly a decade ago
No demonstrated experience with PostgreSQL, AWS/GCP/Azure cloud platforms beyond CDK, Kubernetes depth, or Vitest/Playwright testing frameworks

key_factors.factor: Heavy reliance on DLang across career — a niche language unrelated to the role's stack

response.justification: While the candidate brings substantial years of full-stack experience, the resume reveals significant misalignment with the role's primary stack. Nest.js — a core requirement — is entirely absent, Kotlin is not listed at all, and Java exposure is limited to dated robotic-arm projects. A disproportionate amount of career depth sits in DLang, a niche language with no relevance to this position. Cloud platform breadth (AWS/GCP/Azure), PostgreSQL, Kubernetes operations, and modern testing frameworks like Vitest and Playwright are either unmentioned or only superficially referenced. AI-agent work is recent and shallow rather than demonstrated at depth. The profile reads as a generalist whose recent specialization diverges from the specific stack this role demands.
